jokes.text: Not again. Bow down before Recyclops.
jokes.setup: Corporate mandated recycling program leads to Dwight creating a character
jokes.punchline: The dramatic 'Bow down before Recyclops' delivery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight character comedy - he takes a simple corporate mandate and creates an elaborate fictional character. The dramatic delivery is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Happy Earth Day, everyone. I'm Recyclops. Did you know that an old milk carton can be sawed in half and used as a planter?
jokes.setup: Dwight in costume as environmental superhero
jokes.punchline: The earnest, awkward environmental tip delivery
jokes.explanation: Shows Dwight's commitment to the character and his awkward sincerity. The practical tip makes it more cringe.

jokes.text: Who has put a number seven plastic in a number four bin?
jokes.setup: Evolution of Recyclops character becoming more aggressive
jokes.punchline: The overly specific plastic recycling anger
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Dwight's obsessive attention to detail becoming threatening. The specificity of plastic numbers is perfectly Dwight.

jokes.text: Recyclops will drown you in your over-watered lawns!
jokes.setup: Recyclops becoming increasingly threatening
jokes.punchline: The specific threat about over-watered lawns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - the threat is both menacing and hilariously specific to suburban environmental concerns. Perfect Dwight absurdity.

jokes.text: Then tragedy struck Recyclops when his fictional planet was attacked by some other fictitious thing, I can't remember.
jokes.setup: Building up to a dramatic backstory moment
jokes.punchline: The casual dismissal 'I can't remember'
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor - narrator breaks the fourth wall by admitting they can't remember Dwight's elaborate backstory.

jokes.text: Recyclops will have his revenge!
jokes.setup: Recyclops developing a revenge arc
jokes.punchline: The dramatic villain declaration
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation of Recyclops becoming a villain. Dwight's commitment to the dramatic arc is funny.

jokes.text: I think this was also the year that he renounced Earth Day and vowed to destroy the planet he once loved.
jokes.setup: Environmental character's evolution
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal - now wants to destroy the planet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd escalation - the environmental hero becomes an environmental villain. Perfect ironic reversal.

jokes.text: Oh, is today Recyclops Day? Yes. I thought you were killed by Polluticon or something. Polluticon wishes.
jokes.setup: Jim casually referencing Dwight's elaborate mythology
jokes.punchline: Dwight's serious response about Polluticon
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim/Dwight dynamic - Jim plays along with the mythology while Dwight treats it as completely real. 'Polluticon wishes' is a great tough-guy response.

jokes.text: Humans are terrible for the environment.
jokes.setup: Recyclops discussing environmental damage
jokes.punchline: The blunt, misanthropic statement
jokes.explanation: Typical Dwight misanthropy delivered with complete seriousness. The economy and directness work well.

jokes.text: The thing I like most about Recyclops is that he's creating a different world for our child. A world where you truly can be anything you want. God bless you, Recyclops, and your cold robot heart.
jokes.setup: Pam giving sincere praise to Recyclops
jokes.punchline: 'Your cold robot heart' delivered earnestly
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of sincere parenting sentiment with 'cold robot heart.' Pam treating Dwight's absurd character seriously is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: And it looks like you're taking a dump.
jokes.setup: Michael practicing his humble nod gesture
jokes.punchline: Pam's blunt assessment of how it looks
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Pam's honest assessment deflates Michael's grandiose gesture. Good economy.

jokes.text: And the crowd goes wild. Ladies and gentlemen, from Scranton, Pennsylvania, please welcome Michael 'The Machine' Scott!
jokes.setup: Michael fantasizing about his introduction
jokes.punchline: The over-the-top sports announcer style with 'The Machine'
jokes.explanation: Pure Michael delusion - giving himself a wrestling-style nickname and imagining crowd adoration. Perfectly in character cringe.

jokes.text: Don't do the twirl. Lose the twirl. Twirl sucks. Michael, I hated the twirl. Hate the twirl.
jokes.setup: Michael doing an elaborate entrance gesture
jokes.punchline: The escalating unanimous hatred of the twirl
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with everyone piling on against the twirl. The repetition builds comedy.

jokes.text: It's not even a twirl. It's a spin. I might do the spin.
jokes.setup: Michael seemingly accepting feedback about the twirl
jokes.punchline: The pedantic distinction and immediate backtrack
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic - make a meaningless distinction then do exactly what everyone told him not to. Great character consistency and misdirection.

jokes.text: Okay, Oscar. I don't need the whole enchilada, just bullet points. Those are the bullet points. Well, could you condense it, please? That's as simple as I can make it.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Oscar to simplify complex financial problems
jokes.punchline: Oscar's exasperated 'That's as simple as I can make it'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Michael's inability to grasp complexity and Oscar's growing frustration. Well-structured back-and-forth.

jokes.text: Town cars are actually better, though, better torque, better handling. He said limousine, so... Check it out, guys, there's a limo down here.
jokes.setup: Michael rationalizing why a town car is better
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning that position when he sees the limo
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael hypocrisy - makes up reasons to feel superior then immediately reverses when proven wrong. Good structure.

jokes.text: A limousine is something that a company sends when they have cause for celebration. And in this case, I think we are celebrating me.
jokes.setup: Michael's theory about what limos signify
jokes.punchline: The narcissistic conclusion that they're celebrating him
jokes.explanation: Pure Michael self-aggrandizement. The logical progression from corporate gesture to personal celebration is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Hate to break it to you, Oscar, but some of us like boobs. Calves. Calves all the way.
jokes.setup: Oscar complaining about management spending on 'boobs'
jokes.punchline: Kevin and Creed's literal interpretation about body parts
jokes.explanation: Great misunderstanding comedy - Oscar means 'boobs' as idiots, Kevin takes it literally. Creed's 'calves all the way' is perfectly random.

jokes.text: Hey, you know who you should be jealous of? Yourself. Because you're invited, and you're invited...
jokes.setup: Someone expressing jealousy about the limo
jokes.punchline: Michael's nonsensical 'be jealous of yourself' logic
jokes.explanation: Bizarre Michael logic that makes no sense but sounds profound to him. Good absurdist character comedy.

jokes.text: Dummies, Morons and Idiots, because that's what you'd have to be to own it. And as one of those idiots, I believe the board owes me answers.
jokes.setup: Oscar explaining what DMI stands for
jokes.punchline: Immediately calling himself one of those idiots
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline structure. Oscar insults stockholders then immediately includes himself. Good self-awareness and irony.

jokes.text: That, that's bigger than my bed.
jokes.setup: Characters exploring the luxurious limo
jokes.punchline: Ryan's pathetic living situation revealed
jokes.explanation: Good character detail revealing Ryan's sad post-downfall living situation. Economy works well.

jokes.text: Doesn't seem like there's much of a point if the company's going under. But if the company doesn't go under, then we'll finally have all our contacts in one program. Company's probably going under, though.
jokes.setup: Jim asking Ryan to do data entry work
jokes.punchline: Ryan's circular reasoning to avoid work
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Ryan's lazy avoidance tactics. The circular logic is perfectly frustrating.

jokes.text: Say, your name is Zamboni, and then I will say, 'Well, we're sort of on thin ice.'
jokes.setup: Michael planning softball questions for the meeting
jokes.punchline: The terrible Zamboni/thin ice pun
jokes.explanation: Typical bad Michael pun. The joke is how proud he is of this terrible wordplay. Character integration saves it from being worse.

jokes.text: Oh. It was awesome. Very, very sublime.
jokes.setup: Being asked about the limo ride
jokes.punchline: Michael's pretentious use of 'sublime'
jokes.explanation: Michael trying to sound sophisticated but coming off as trying too hard. Mild character comedy.

jokes.text: Your eminence.
jokes.setup: Being introduced to former congressman
jokes.punchline: Michael's inappropriate formal address
jokes.explanation: Michael's awkward attempt to be respectful using religious terminology for a politician. Good cringe moment.

jokes.text: Look, do you want to be able to tell your grandkids you stood up for yourself during America's biggest financial crisis? How is he going to have grandkids?
jokes.setup: Jim encouraging Oscar to speak up at the meeting
jokes.punchline: Pam's brutal assessment of Oscar's prospects
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline with Jim's inspiring speech immediately deflated by Pam's practical observation. Sharp and unexpected.

jokes.text: Not supposed to talk about it. Nelly Furtado.
jokes.setup: Security guard saying they can't discuss celebrity protection
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals protecting Nelly Furtado
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - builds up professional discretion then immediately breaks it. Nelly Furtado is an unexpectedly specific choice.

jokes.text: If onlys and justs were candies and nuts then every day would be Erntedankfest.
jokes.setup: Someone making excuses about bathroom needs
jokes.punchline: Dwight's mangled saying with German holiday
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - takes a common saying and makes it incomprehensibly German. 'Erntedankfest' (German Thanksgiving) is perfectly random.

jokes.text: I'm a co-manager. That doesn't make you a boss. It's not like you can fire people or anything.
jokes.setup: Jim asserting his authority as co-manager
jokes.punchline: Phyllis deflating his authority completely
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Jim's attempt at authority. Phyllis cuts right to the heart of his powerlessness.

jokes.text: Who... How did you... Who told you this? We can't say. Ryan. Sorry. I'm plastered.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to find the source of rumors about his authority
jokes.punchline: Ryan immediately confessing while drunk
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure - Phyllis tries to protect the source, then Ryan immediately gives himself away. His drunk honesty is in character.

jokes.text: Got over 500 students to just skip the SATs. At the last second, I chickened out, took it anyway. Got a 1220. Always regretted it.
jokes.setup: Oscar describing organizing a rebellion in high school
jokes.punchline: He chickened out and took the test, got a decent score
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar character moment - talks up his rebellion then reveals his practical, cautious nature. The specific score adds authenticity.

jokes.text: I feel lachrymose.
jokes.setup: Oscar expressing his emotional state
jokes.punchline: Using an unnecessarily fancy word for 'sad'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar pretension - using 'lachrymose' instead of 'sad.' Shows his intellectual showing-off tendency.

jokes.text: They invited me to their hospitality suite, where I had free food and it was delicious. Get this, you know how nice they are? They sent a stretch limo all the way to pick me up in Scranton.
jokes.setup: Angry shareholders confronting corrupt executives
jokes.punchline: Michael defending them because they gave him perks
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael cringe - defending potentially corrupt executives because they treated him well. Perfectly selfish and naive.

jokes.text: Do you love her, or do you love the idea of her? I don't know, man. I just don't know.
jokes.setup: Serious corporate financial crisis meeting
jokes.punchline: Random relationship advice conversation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist juxtaposition - while the company burns, random people are having deep relationship talks. Original but lacks character integration.

jokes.text: Like the time you said, 'Hey, look, we parked over here!' Well, that was apple-picking day. There was no need to yell that day. You know, I was just excited to find the car. Perfect end to a perfect day.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Jim's inability to yell at people
jokes.punchline: Jim's wholesome memory of apple-picking day
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Jim's gentle nature. The apple-picking detail and his happy memory contrasts with the corporate crisis.

jokes.text: It's a 45-day, 45-point, one point per day, we get to 45 points, we're back in business! And you can take that to the bank!
jokes.setup: Angry shareholders demanding a plan
jokes.punchline: Michael's nonsensical 45-day/45-point mathematical solution
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael fake-expertise moment. The mathematical precision of his made-up plan is perfectly absurd. 'Take that to the bank' adds beautiful confidence.

jokes.text: And, limo lady, we are going completely carbon-neutral! I love you, New York!
jokes.setup: Michael making promises to angry crowd
jokes.punchline: Random environmental promise and rock-star ending
jokes.explanation: Michael's complete misread of the room, ending with rock-concert-style crowd love. The environmental promise is random but in character.

jokes.text: I'm such a perfectionist that I'd kind of rather not do it at all than do a crappy version.
jokes.setup: Ryan avoiding simple data entry work
jokes.punchline: The pseudo-intellectual excuse for laziness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan character comedy - turning laziness into fake intellectual superiority. The pseudo-deep reasoning is perfectly pretentious.

jokes.text: Visual gag: Jim putting Ryan in the annex closet as his 'new office'
jokes.setup: Jim dealing with Ryan's work avoidance
jokes.punchline: The tiny closet presented as an office upgrade
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comedy with Jim's passive-aggressive solution to Ryan's attitude. The 'promotion' to a closet is perfectly Jim.

jokes.text: Is there Internet?
jokes.setup: Ryan locked in tiny closet office
jokes.punchline: His only concern is internet access
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan priority - not concerned about the workspace conditions, just his internet access. Great economy.

jokes.text: That wasn't what you were saying to me. He was much more articulate and that was better, much better.
jokes.setup: Oscar giving a bland, safe presentation
jokes.punchline: Michael undermining Oscar in front of executives
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael sabotage through helpful intent. He makes Oscar look bad while trying to make him look good.

jokes.text: Where's the off button on this moron?
jokes.setup: Michael continuing to talk despite clear signals to stop
jokes.punchline: Executive's brutal assessment of Michael
jokes.explanation: Harsh but effective putdown. The 'off button' metaphor treats Michael like a malfunctioning device.

jokes.text: I have personally won over 17 Dundie Awards, so I am not a moron.
jokes.setup: Michael defending his intelligence
jokes.punchline: Using self-awarded fake awards as proof
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael delusion - using awards he created and gave to himself as proof of competence. The specificity of '17' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Dwight Schrute, Scranton Branch. I just want to say that I have been standing in this line all day.
jokes.setup: Dwight finally getting to speak at the shareholder meeting
jokes.punchline: His complaint is about the line, not corporate malfeasance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anticlimax - while everyone else discusses bankruptcy, Dwight focuses on operational inefficiency. Perfectly in character priority.

jokes.text: A take-a-number option, like they have in a deli. What about line varieties? Like an express line for quick comments of 10 words or less.
jokes.setup: Dwight suggesting line management solutions
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific queue management ideas
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight obsession with efficiency and systems. While the company burns, he's designing better queuing systems. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: What about ropes along the lines that you can hold on to?
jokes.setup: Dwight continuing his line management suggestions
jokes.punchline: The increasingly mundane rope suggestion
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation of Dwight's practical suggestions. The rope idea is so mundane it becomes funny.
